Always A Runner charges late to win Kentucky Oaks

items.summary: Always a Runner, a filly trained by Chad Brown, has won the Kentucky Oaks. This victory marks Brown's first Kentucky Oaks win, thanks to a strong push down the final stretch by Always a Runner. The win came on May 1, 2026.

supporting_articles.title: Unmasking Voter Fraud: Four Noncitizens Charged with Illegal Voting in NJ Elections
Four noncitizens charged with illegally voting in 2020, 2022 and 2024 federal elections in New Jersey
Four foreign nationals in NJ charged in connection with illegally voting in federal elections: DOJ
DOJ Indicts Four Noncitizens for Alleged Illegal Voting in Federal Election
Kash Patel Reveals 4 Noncitizens Charged with Illegally Voting in Elections

items.summary: The US Department of Justice has indicted four noncitizens in New Jersey for allegedly voting illegally in federal elections and providing false information during their US citizenship applications. The individuals, from Liberia, Jamaica, Israel, and India, are charged with various crimes, including voting by an alien in a federal election and making false statements in relation to naturalization. If convicted, they face prison sentences ranging from 10 to 16 years.

supporting_articles.title: Jarren Duran hits 3-run HR, Jake Bennett wins big-league debut as Red Sox beat Astros 3-1

Jarren Duran, Jake Bennett help Red Sox knock off Astros
Jarren Duran hits 3-run HR, Jake Bennett wins big-league debut as Red Sox beat Astros 3-1
Jarren Duran, Jake Bennett help Red Sox knock off Astros

items.summary: The Boston Red Sox beat the Houston Astros 3-1 on Friday night, with Jarren Duran hitting a three-run homer and Jake Bennett earning the win in his major league debut. Bennett, a 25-year-old left-hander, gave up a run on five hits over five innings, while Duran's homer in the third inning put the Red Sox ahead 3-1. Aroldis Chapman secured the win with his sixth save of the season.

supporting_articles.title: Mitchell Robinson, Dyson Daniels Fined by NBA, How Much Did Players Lose From Knicks-Hawks Scuffle?
NBA Announces Punishments for Mitchell Robinson vs. Dyson Daniels Scuffle
Mitchell Robinson, Dyson Daniels Fined By NBA
NBA fines Mitchell Robinson, Dyson Daniels for tussle
NBA fines Mitchell Robinson, Dyson Daniels for tussle

items.summary: The NBA has announced punishments for the scuffle between Mitchell Robinson and Dyson Daniels, with Robinson receiving a $50,000 fine and Daniels a $25,000 fine. The fines were handed down due to the altercation during Game 6 between the New York Knicks and Atlanta Hawks, which resulted in technical fouls and ejections for both players. Robinson's higher fine was attributed to a postgame social media post in which he harshly trolled Daniels.

items.title: Advocates Bash 'Junk Science' As Appeals Court Blocks Abortion Pill by Mail
items.text_preview: Pro-choice advocates told Newsweek that a decision in a New Orleans appeals court that blocked the distribution of mifepristone through the mail is "one step closer to a national abortion ban." Mini...

supporting_articles.title: Advocates Bash 'Junk Science' As Appeals Court Blocks Abortion Pill by Mail
Federal appeals court blocks mailing of abortion pills in ruling with nationwide effect
Federal court blocks access to abortion pills by mail
US appeals court blocks mail-order access to abortion drugs

items.summary: A New Orleans appeals court has blocked the distribution of the abortion pill mifepristone by mail, citing a lack of basis in data and scientific literature for the FDA's decision to allow mail distribution. Pro-choice advocates have criticized the decision, calling it "junk science" and a step towards limiting abortion access. The decision is a temporary measure that remains in effect while the court considers the challenge, which could take months to resolve.
